name: Maria Hernandez Park playground + loops
ageFit: Best for: easy, low-lift toddler energy burn
summary: Closest reset button: two playground zones, lots of stroller space, and room for a snack lap when you want maximum payoff for minimum logistics.
plan: Walk over, do playground first, then one slow loop around the park before heading home for nap.
tips: Good fallback when weather is decent but everyone is tired.

name: Brooklyn Children’s Museum
summary: The safest all-weather pick. The toddler-centered spaces make it easy to let a small kid roam without overcomplicating the day.
plan: Aim for an early slot, spend most of the visit in Totally Tots / sensory-forward areas, then do a short snack break before the ride back.
tips: Easy to keep the outing to 90 minutes if needed.
Bring a change of clothes if your kid loves hands-on play.

name: New York Transit Museum
ageFit: Best for: train-obsessed toddlers and parents who want an easy indoor win
summary: Big payoff if your kid likes buses, trains, buttons, maps, or anything with wheels. The vintage subway cars are usually the highlight.
plan: Keep expectations simple: one pass through the exhibits, extra time in the train cars, then a snack nearby.
tips: Very good backup for messy weather.
Can feel exciting without needing a full-day commitment.
Best if your kid already loves transit.

name: Prospect Park Zoo
summary: Compact enough for toddler attention spans, with enough animal payoff to feel like a real outing instead of a chore.
plan: Do the animal buildings first while attention is highest, then finish with a playground or park walk if energy allows.
tips: Pair well with a short Prospect Park loop.
Good balance of outdoor and indoor time.
Easier than a giant zoo with a stroller.

name: Jane’s Carousel + Brooklyn Bridge Park
ageFit: Best for: scenic outing with one clear toddler hook
summary: Carousel ride, waterfront views, and enough room for a stroller-friendly wander. Good when the adults also want the outing to feel pleasant.
plan: Ride the carousel early, then walk the park and let the toddler lead the pace.
tips: Easy to pair with coffee/snack for the grown-up.
Less ideal in wind or cold.
